title: Marvell Stock Is Up 230% In 2026 — S&P 500 Debut Could Make That Look Conservative
author: Erica Kollmann
description: Marvell Technology (MRVL) has surged 230% in 2026 and will be added to the S&P 500 effective June 22, which could drive further gains through forced institutional buying. The semiconductor designer benefits from strong AI infrastructure demand. However, recent S&P 500 inclusions show mixed results—while data center infrastructure companies like Comfort Systems USA and EMCOR Group have surged post-inclusion, ad-tech and software names like The Trade Desk have cratered.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Up 230% YTD with strong positioning in AI silicon and networking. S&P 500 inclusion on June 22 will trigger billions in programmatic buying. Nvidia CEO endorsed it as potential $1 trillion market cap candidate. Fundamentals remain strong with sustained hyperscaler demand.

Surged 91.3% since S&P 500 inclusion on Dec 22, 2025. Benefits from AI data center buildout as HVAC and electrical contractor, demonstrating strong post-inclusion performance in the infrastructure sector.
Up 76.4% since September 2025 S&P 500 inclusion. Electrical and mechanical contractor with heavy data center exposure, benefiting from the AI infrastructure wave.
Gained nearly 20% since March 2026 S&P 500 inclusion. Supplies power infrastructure to data centers, positioned to benefit from ongoing AI buildout.
Cratered 75.1% since July 2025 S&P 500 inclusion, the worst-performing recent addition. Demonstrates that index inclusion alone doesn't guarantee performance when underlying business faces headwinds.
Down 47.2% since December 2024 S&P 500 inclusion, illustrating that software names have underperformed post-inclusion despite index tailwinds.
Fallen 42.1% since May 2025 S&P 500 inclusion, showing that even high-profile additions can underperform if business conditions deteriorate.

title: This Infrastructure Stock Is Up 124% This Past Year, and One Fund Dumped Its $38 Million Stake Last Quarter
author: Jonathan Ponciano
description: Goodlander Investment Management exited its entire $37.77 million position in Primoris (PRIM) during Q4 2025, selling 275,000 shares. Despite the stock surging 124% over the past year and the company delivering solid growth with 19% revenue increase and 22% EBITDA growth, the fund's exit appears to be profit-taking after the dramatic rally rather than a rejection of the infrastructure theme, as the fund maintains other infrastructure and industrial holdings.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: While the company shows strong fundamentals with 19% revenue growth and 22% EBITDA growth, the stock's 124% surge has created lofty valuation expectations. The fund's exit is characterized as profit-taking rather than a fundamental concern, suggesting the company remains sound but may face near-term headwinds from stretched valuations after its dramatic rally.
Mentioned as a top holding and recommended by The Motley Fool, indicating positive regard, though the article provides no specific performance metrics or analysis.
Listed as a top holding in the fund's portfolio and recommended by The Motley Fool, suggesting confidence in the infrastructure/engineering services sector, though no specific analysis is provided.
Held in the fund's portfolio and recommended by The Motley Fool, indicating positive positioning in the renewable energy/infrastructure space, though no specific performance data is discussed.

Listed as a top holding in Goodlander's portfolio at $44.13 million (16.9% of AUM), indicating the fund's continued confidence in this infrastructure/industrial company, though no specific performance data is provided.

title: This Energy Services Stock Up Nearly 100% Got a $36 Million Institutional Bet Last Quarter
author: Jonathan Ponciano
description: Goodlander Investment Management made a significant $36 million investment in Liberty Energy (LBRT) by purchasing 1.95 million shares in Q4 2025, making it a top-5 holding in their portfolio. Liberty Energy shares have surged 96% over the past year, driven by strong revenue of $4 billion and $148 million in net income in 2025, with the company expanding into distributed power solutions for data centers and industrial users.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong institutional backing with a $36M investment from Goodlander, exceptional stock performance (96% YoY gain), solid financial metrics ($4B revenue, $148M net income), and strategic expansion into emerging markets like distributed power solutions for data centers.
Mentioned as a top holding in Goodlander's portfolio (14% of AUM) but no specific news or analysis provided about the company itself.
Listed as a top holding in Goodlander's portfolio (11.2% of AUM) but no specific news or analysis provided about the company itself.

title: Tom Gardner's 2026 Outlook: Volatility, Government Scrutiny, and Two AI-Adjacent Stocks
author: Motley Fool Youtube
description: Tom Gardner, Motley Fool co-founder and CEO, outlines his 2026 investment outlook emphasizing market volatility preparedness and a diversified portfolio strategy. He predicts increased government intervention in large tech companies due to energy consumption and data privacy concerns, recommending investors diversify away from mega-cap tech. He highlights EMCOR and Moderna as beneficiaries of AI trends while maintaining a disciplined approach of holding 25+ stocks for 5+ years.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Gardner highlights EMCOR as a beneficiary of continued data center buildout despite increased energy costs. The company installs critical mechanical and electrical systems for data centers and is positioned to benefit from AI infrastructure growth over the next 5 years.

Gardner recommends Moderna as a diversification play away from large tech. He believes biotech companies like Moderna will benefit from AI-accelerated drug discovery, regulatory processes, and clinical timelines. The stock has already more than doubled in recent months with a bright 5-year outlook.

title: This $8 Million ETF Buy Extends a Bond Ladder Spanning 2026 Through 2031
author: Jonathan Ponciano
description: Indiana-based Kirr Marbach acquired 466,959 shares of the Invesco BulletShares 2031 Corporate Bond ETF (BSCV) worth approximately $7.80 million in Q4, extending its bond ladder strategy across maturities from 2026 through 2031. The move provides predictable cash flow and flexibility while maintaining investment-grade corporate bond exposure.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The ETF is being actively acquired as part of a strategic bond ladder extension, demonstrating investor confidence in its investment-grade corporate bond portfolio, competitive yield (4.7%), and transparent structure for managing maturity risk.
Listed as a top holding in Kirr Marbach's portfolio (7.1% of AUM), but the article provides no specific commentary on the company's performance or outlook.
